creator: pkubaj@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 204055
patch

Clang thinks FreeBSD uses libc++ everywhere and tries to use it. This doesn't work on GCC architectures, there's no Clang there. To compile with Clang, one needs to pass -stdlib=libstdc++ and appriopriate flags to libstdc++ from GCC from ports (libstdc++ from base is too old).

With this patch, you can simply use -stdlib=libstdc++ and GCC will be found automatically, making building with clang much easier on GCC architectures.

Tested on powerpc64.
